content: What is the general guidelines for using something like core::intrinsics::unlikely()? It is marked 'perma-unstable' :shrug: But it is easier to read than marking a target function #[cold].

I have it in our wishlist (https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/issues/354). The upstream tracking issue is https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/26179 -- it seems they might expose it eventually in std::hint.
The latest updates from the Rust team(s) talk about doing so as an attribute #[likely], though.
Do the intrinsics work for you, by the way? There are also some reports of not having an effect.

Miguel Ojeda said:

Do the intrinsics work for you, by the way? There are also some reports of not having an effect.

I did not check the compiler output, but now I will :sweat_smile:

Miguel Ojeda said:

Do the intrinsics work for you, by the way? There are also some reports of not having an effect.

#[cold] is working fine but core::intrinsics::unlikely() does not have an effect for me :shrug:
